Reading Mode
This page keeps the whole session intact while making long transcripts easier to scan.
  • Messages stay in left/right conversation bubbles.
  • Tool calls and results are folded by default.
  • Reasoning steps remain visible without leaking encrypted content.
  • Context, task state, and token counters stay in the timeline for auditability.

Conversation Flow
Use the controls to focus on one slice of the session: human prompts, assistant output, tool traffic, reasoning markers, or system context. The raw structure is preserved without forcing everything into one flat blob.

Implement the following plan: # Plan: `memorytree report` — Full HTML Memory Website ## Context The user wants to export the entire MemoryTree workspace (transcripts, goals, knowledge) as a self-contained multi-page H...

Expand full message

Implement the following plan:

# Plan: memorytree report — Full HTML Memory Website

## Context

The user wants to export the entire MemoryTree workspace (transcripts, goals, knowledge) as a
self-contained multi-page HTML website, generated by the heartbeat daemon. Statistics are computed
purely by code; only per-session summaries call the Claude API (cached by raw_sha256 so tokens are
spent only once per transcript). The output must work from file:// with zero external CDN
dependencies — all SVG charts are rendered inline.

---

## Architecture


src/
  types/report.ts           ← ReportStats, SummaryCache, LinkGraph, SearchIndexEntry, BuildReportOptions
  report/
    build.ts                ← orchestrator, exports buildReport()
    stats.ts                ← computeStats(), buildDayBuckets(), extractToolNames()
    summarize.ts            ← getSummary() + cache read/write + buildSummaryPrompt()
    render/
      css.ts                ← REPORT_CSS string constant (GitHub Dark palette + CSS vars)
      layout.ts             ← htmlShell(), renderNav(), escHtml()
      charts.ts             ← renderHeatmap(), renderClientDoughnut(), renderWeeklyLine(), renderToolBar()
      dashboard.ts          ← renderDashboard(stats, manifests)
      transcript-list.ts    ← renderTranscriptList(manifests)
      transcript.ts         ← renderTranscript(parsed, manifest, summary, backlinks)
      knowledge.ts          ← renderKnowledge(files)
      goals.ts              ← renderGoals(files)
      search.ts             ← buildSearchIndex() + renderSearchPage()
      links.ts              ← buildLinkGraph()
  cli/
    cmd-report.ts           ← thin CLI adapter: cmdReportBuild, cmdReportServe

Modified files:
  src/cli/main.ts           ← register 'report build' and 'report serve' subcommands
  src/heartbeat/config.ts   ← add generate_report: boolean, ai_summary_model: string
  src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts← call buildReport() after gitCommitAndPush if config.generate_report
  package.json              ← add @anthropic-ai/sdk dependency

---

## Output Structure


Memory/07_reports/          ← .gitignored, never committed
  index.html                ← Dashboard
  transcripts/
    index.html              ← Session list
    codex/{stem}.html       ← Individual Codex sessions
    claude/{stem}.html      ← Individual Claude sessions
    gemini/{stem}.html      ← Individual Gemini sessions
  goals/index.html
  knowledge/index.html
  search.html               ← Full-text search (index embedded as inline JS)
  .cache/{sha256}.json      ← AI summary cache files

---

## Pages

### index.html — Dashboard
Stats cards (code only):
- Total sessions / messages / tool events / active days
- Date range

SVG charts (all inline, no CDN):
- GitHub-style activity heatmap (last 365 days, 5-level green scale)
- Client distribution doughnut (codex=blue, claude=purple, gemini=green)
- Messages/week line chart (last 52 weeks, filled area)
- Top 10 tools horizontal bar chart

Recent sessions table (last 10, linked).

### transcripts/{client}/{stem}.html — Individual Session
- Metadata header (client badge, date, duration, message count)
- AI summary card (2–3 sentences, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, cached)
- Backlinks: "Referenced by N sessions"
- Full conversation (user=blue bubble, assistant=gray, code blocks with monospace)

### search.html — Full-text Search
- Pre-built JSON index embedded as inline const SEARCH_INDEX = [...]
- Vanilla JS substring search, no external library
- Results link to individual session pages

### goals/ and knowledge/ pages
- Render raw Markdown content as HTML (simple regex-based Markdown → HTML converter,
no external lib needed for the subset used: headers, bold, italic, code, lists)

---

## SVG Charts (self-contained, no Chart.js)

| Chart | Technique |
|-------|-----------|
| Heatmap | 53×7 <rect> grid, 5 color levels, <title> tooltip |
| Doughnut | SVG arc paths, 3 segments, legend below |
| Line | <path> filled area + stroke line |
| Bar | Horizontal <rect> rows, label + count text |

Edge cases: all-zero data renders gracefully (empty ring, flat line, gray cells).

---

## AI Summary


// Only called when: cache miss AND ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set AND noAi=false
const response = await client.messages.create({
  model: options?.model ?? 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
  max_tokens: 200,
  system: 'Summarize in 2-3 sentences. What was accomplished? What problems solved? Be specific. Past tense.',
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: buildSummaryPrompt(messages) }],
})
// buildSummaryPrompt: first 30 messages, each truncated to 500 chars
// Cache: Memory/07_reports/.cache/{sha256}.json
// On any error: log warning, return '' (never crash build)
// SDK imported dynamically: await import('@anthropic-ai/sdk')

---

## Config Changes (src/heartbeat/config.ts)


// New fields in Config interface:
readonly generate_report: boolean          // default: false
readonly ai_summary_model: string          // default: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'

Add to defaultConfig(), parseRaw(), and saveConfig().

---

## Heartbeat Integration (src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts)

After gitCommitAndPush() in processProject():


if (config.generate_report) {
  try {
    const { buildReport } = await import('../report/build.js')
    await buildReport({
      root: projectPath,
      output: join(projectPath, 'Memory', '07_reports'),
      noAi: !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'],
      pdf: false,
    })
    logger.info(`[${projectName}] Report generated.`)
  } catch (err) {
    logger.warn(`[${projectName}] Report generation failed: ${String(err)}`)
    // Never propagate — report failure must not abort heartbeat cycle
  }
}

---

## Key Implementation Notes

1. HTML escaping: All user content interpolated into HTML must go through escHtml() — handles & < > " '. Critical for XSS prevention.

2. Path resolution: manifest.repo_clean_path is POSIX-relative, resolve with join(root, path). Fall back to global_clean_path if repo path is empty or missing.

3. --no-ai flag gotcha: Commander's --no-ai sets opts.ai = false. Map to noAi: !opts.ai.

4. Memory efficiency: Process transcripts one at a time in build.ts — parse → summarize → render → write → discard. Keep only manifests in memory throughout.

5. Parallel AI calls: Max 5 concurrent summary requests (simple counter semaphore, no extra deps).

6. .gitignore: buildReport() checks if Memory/07_reports/ is in .gitignore and appends it if not.

7. @anthropic-ai/sdk optional: Dynamic import with MODULE_NOT_FOUND catch → returns ''. Build never fails without the package.

8. Serve command: Uses node:http built-in, no extra dep. EADDRINUSE → clear error + exit 1.

---

## New Dependency


"@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.39.0"

---

## Tests

| File | Type | Key cases |
|------|------|-----------|
| tests/report/stats.test.ts | Unit | zero state, day/week bucketing, tool name extraction, client counts |
| tests/report/render/charts.test.ts | Unit | empty data, single point, >10 tools, all-zero doughnut |
| tests/report/render/links.test.ts | Unit | [[id]] detection, self-ref ignored, partial bracket no match |
| tests/report/render/search.test.ts | Unit | 50kB truncation, valid JSON embed, correct URL format |
| tests/report/summarize.test.ts | Unit | cache hit, noAi skip, missing API key, 30-msg + 500-char truncation |
| tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts | Unit | new fields read/default/round-trip |
| tests/report/build.test.ts | Integration | scaffold temp Memory/, call buildReport, assert all HTML files exist + contain <svg |

---

## Implementation Order

1. src/types/report.ts
2. src/report/render/css.ts + layout.ts (escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav)
3. src/report/stats.ts + tests
4. src/report/render/charts.ts + tests
5. src/report/render/links.ts + search.ts + tests
6. src/report/render/dashboard.ts, transcript-list.ts, transcript.ts, knowledge.ts, goals.ts
7. src/report/summarize.ts + tests
8. src/report/build.ts + integration test
9. src/cli/cmd-report.ts + register in main.ts
10. Modify config.ts + heartbeat.ts + tests
11. package.json dependency + rebuild

---

## Verification


# 1. Install new dependency
cd C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow && npm install

# 2. Build
npm run build

# 3. Run all tests (must stay 401+ passing, 0 failing)
npm test

# 4. Manual end-to-end
node dist/cli.js report build --root D:/demo1/openmnemo --no-ai
# → Memory/07_reports/ created, open index.html in browser

# 5. With AI summaries (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-... node dist/cli.js report build --root D:/demo1/openmnemo
# → .cache/{sha256}.json files created, transcripts show summary card

# 6. Heartbeat integration
# Set generate_report = true in ~/.memorytree/config.toml
node dist/cli.js daemon run-once
# → Report auto-generated after transcript import

**Type Definitions** (`src/types/transcript.ts`)\n\n```typescript\nexport type Client = 'codex' | 'claude' | 'gemini'\n\nexport interface TranscriptMessage {\n  role: string                    // 'user', 'assistant', etc.\n  text: string\n  timestamp: string | null        // ISO 8601 or null\n}\n\nexport interface TranscriptToolEvent {\n  summary: string                 // e.g., \"function_call input=...\"\n  timestamp: string | null\n}\n\nexport interface ParsedTranscript {\n  client: Client\n  session_id: string\n  title: string\n  started_at: string             // ISO 8601\n  cwd: string                    // Current working directory\n  branch: string                 // Git branch name\n  messages: TranscriptMessage[]\n  tool_events: TranscriptToolEvent[]\n  source_path: string            // Original file path\n}\n\nexport interface ManifestEntry {\n  client: string\n  project: string                // Project slug\n  session_id: string\n  raw_sha256: string\n  title: string\n  started_at: string\n  imported_at: string\n  cwd: string\n  branch: string\n  raw_source_path: string\n  raw_upload_permission: string  // 'not-set', 'approved', 'denied'\n  global_raw_path: string        // ~/.memorytree/transcripts/raw/...\n  global_clean_path: string      // ~/.memorytree/transcripts/clean/...\n  global_manifest_path: string   // ~/.memorytree/transcripts/index/manifests/...\n  repo_raw_path: string          // Memory/06_transcripts/raw/...\n  repo_clean_path: string        // Memory/06_transcripts/clean/...\n  repo_manifest_path: string     // Memory/06_transcripts/manifests/...\n  message_count: number\n  tool_event_count: number\n  cleaning_mode: string          // 'deterministic-code'\n  repo_mirror_enabled: boolean\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **Core Files**\n\n#### **package.json**\n- Name: `memorytree-workflow` v0.1.0\n- Node: >=20\n- Binaries: `memorytree`, `memorytree-daemon` → `dist/cli.js`\n- Key Dependencies:\n  - `commander` ^13.1.0 (CLI framework)\n  - `fast-glob` ^3.3.3 (file discovery)\n  - `smol-toml` ^1.3.1 (config parsing)\n  - `sql.js` ^1.12.0 (SQLite search index)\n- Dev: vitest, tsx, tsup, typescript, eslint\n\n#### **tsconfig.json**\n- Target: ES2022\n- Module: NodeNext\n- Strict mode enabled\n- exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noUncheckedIndexedAccess for extra safety\n- sourceMap: true\n\n#### **src/heartbeat/config.ts** (236 lines)\n**Purpose**: Manage ~/.memorytree/config.toml configuration\n\n**Key Functions**:\n- `loadConfig(): Config` - Load TOML with fallback to defaults\n- `saveConfig(cfg: Config): void` - Persist config to TOML file\n- `intervalToSeconds(interval: string): number` - Parse \"5m\" → 300 seconds\n- `registerProject(cfg: Config, repoPath: string): Config` - Add project (immutable update)\n\n**Config Interface**:\n```typescript\ninterface Config {\n  heartbeat_interval: string      // '5m', '1h', etc. Default: '5m'\n  watch_dirs: readonly string[]   // Directories to monitor\n  projects: readonly ProjectEntry[]\n  auto_push: boolean              // Default: true\n  log_level: string               // 'debug'|'info'|'warn'|'error'\n}\n```\n\n**Key Implementation Details**:\n- Uses smol-toml for parsing\n- Defaults: 5m interval, info level, auto_push enabled\n- Validates intervals with regex `/^(\\d+)\\s*(s|m|h)$/`\n- Deduplicates projects by resolved path\n- **Immutable**: registerProject returns new Config object\n\n#### **src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts** (209 lines)\n**Purpose**: Main heartbeat daemon orchestration\n\n**Entry Points**:\n- `main(): Promise<number>` - Single execution with lock acquisition\n- `runHeartbeat(config: Config): Promise<number>` - Orchestrate project processing\n- `processProject(config, projectPath, projectName): Promise<void>` - Per-project work\n\n**Core Logic**:\n1. Acquire lock (prevent concurrent runs)\n2. For each registered project:\n   - Discover source transcripts (Codex, Claude, Gemini)\n   - Parse each transcript\n   - Check for content\n   - Match to repository\n   - Scan for sensitive patterns\n   - Import to Memory/\n3. Git commit + push (with retry)\n4. Release lock\n\n**Sensitive Pattern Detection** (5 regex patterns):\n- API keys: `api_key=` or `apikey=`\n- Passwords: `password=`, `passwd=`, `pwd=`\n- Secrets/tokens: `secret=`, `token=`\n- Known prefixes: `sk-`, `pk_live_`, `ghp_`, `gho_`, `glpat-`\n- Bearer tokens: `Bearer <20+ chars>`\n\n**Git Operations**:\n- Check `git status --porcelain Memory/` for changes\n- Commit with message: `memorytree(transcripts): import N transcript(s)`\n- Push with retry logic (2 attempts)\n- Threshold-based alerts on 3 consecutive failures\n\n#### **src/cli/main.ts** (209 lines)\n**Purpose**: CLI entry point with all subcommands\n\n**Commands**:\n1. `init` - Initialize MemoryTree in repository\n   - Options: --root, --project-name, --goal-summary, --locale, --date, --time, --force\n2. `upgrade` - Upgrade repo without overwriting policy\n3. `import` - Import single transcript\n   - Required: --source\n   - Options: --root, --client, --project-name, --global-root, --raw-upload-permission\n4. `discover` - Bulk import with filtering\n   - Options: --client (all|codex|claude|gemini), --scope, --limit\n5. `locale` - Detect effective locale\n6. `recall` - On-demand sync + session recall\n7. `daemon` (subcommands):\n   - `install` - Register with OS scheduler\n   - `uninstall` - Remove scheduled task\n   - `run-once` - Single heartbeat cycle\n   - `watch` - Continuous loop (dev only)\n   - `status` - Show registration state\n\n**Implementation**: Uses Commander.js with lazy import of command handlers\n\n### 4. **Supporting Files**\n\n#### **src/heartbeat/log.ts** (203 lines)\n- Singleton logger writing to `~/.memorytree/logs/heartbeat-YYYY-MM-DD.log` + stderr\n- Levels: debug, info, warn, error\n- Methods: debug(), info(), warn(), error(), exception(msg, err)\n- Test helper: `_resetLogger()`\n\n#### **src/heartbeat/alert.ts** (216 lines)\n- Persistent deduplication: `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`\n- Threshold-based: accumulate 3 failures before writing alert\n- Failure counts: `~/.memorytree/failure_counts.json`\n- Max alerts kept: 100 (FIFO trim)\n- Alert types: no_remote, sensitive_match, push_failed, lock_held\n- Functions: readAlerts(), writeAlert(), writeAlertWithThreshold(), resetFailureCount()\n\n#### **src/heartbeat/lock.ts** (170 lines)\n- PID-based exclusive lock: `~/.memorytree/heartbeat.lock`\n- Handles stale locks from dead processes\n- Platform detection: Windows (tasklist) vs Unix (kill -0)\n- TOCTOU-safe: uses `O_CREAT | O_EXCL`\n- Functions: acquireLock(), releaseLock(), readLockPid(), isProcessAlive()\n\n#### **src/transcript/parse.ts** (398 lines)\n**Three parsers** (by client):\n\n**Codex Parser** (parseCodexTranscript):\n- JSONL format with canonical `response_item` records\n- Fallback to `event_msg` streaming events\n- Filters system injections with markers: `<INSTRUCTIONS>`, `<environment_context>`, etc.\n- Extracts: messages, tool events (function_call, custom_tool_call)\n\n**Claude Parser** (parseClaudeTranscript):\n- JSONL records with structured content blocks\n- Supports text, thinking, reasoning (skipped), tool_use, tool_result\n- Deduplicates by timestamp + content hash\n\n**Gemini Parser** (parseGeminiTranscript):\n- Flexible: JSONL or JSON\n- Deep tree traversal to find metadata and messages\n- Handles variants: role/author/sender, parts/content/text, toolUse/toolName\n\n**Common**: Client inference from path patterns (/.codex/, /.claude/, /.gemini/)\n\n#### **src/transcript/common.ts** (356 lines)\n**Utilities**:\n- `slugify(value, fallback)` - Convert to kebab-case slug\n- `sha256File(path)` - Hash raw file\n- `contentHash(text)` - First 16 chars of SHA256\n- `normalizeTimestamp(value, fallback)` - Convert to ISO 8601\n- `parseIsoTimestamp(value)` - Parse ISO to Date\n- `timestampPartition(ts)` - Extract YYYY, MM for directory paths\n- `joinParagraphs(parts)` - Join text with double newlines\n- `summarizeValue(value, limit)` - Summarize to 180 chars\n- `yamlEscape(value)` - Quote & escape for YAML front matter\n- `deduplicateMessages()`, `deduplicateToolEvents()` - By signature (timestamp + hash)\n- Text extraction: `extractTextBlocks()`, `extractSimpleText()`, `extractGeminiText()`\n- Data access: `ensureDict()`, `ensureList()`, `getNested()`, `loadJsonl()`, `loadJson()`\n\n#### **src/transcript/import.ts** (150+ lines)\n**Main Function**: `importTranscript(parsed, root, globalRoot, projectSlug, permission, mirrorToRepo)`\n\n**Creates Artifacts**:\n1. Raw JSONL copy (global + optionally repo)\n2. Clean markdown with YAML front matter (global + optionally repo)\n3. JSON manifest (global + optionally repo)\n4. Appends to `sessions.jsonl` (deduped by SHA256)\n5. Upserts SQLite search index\n\n**Paths**:\n- Repo: `Memory/06_transcripts/{raw,clean,manifests}/{client}/{YYYY}/{MM}/{session_id}__{hash8}.{ext}`\n- Global: `~/.memorytree/transcripts/{raw,clean}/{client}/{project}/{YYYY}/{MM}/{session_id}__{hash8}.{ext}`\n\n**Markdown Format**: YAML front matter + metadata table + messages + tool events\n\n### 5. **Memory Directory Structure** (D:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory)\n\n```\nMemory/\n├── 01_goals/                          # Long-term project goals\n│   └── goal_v001_20260316.md\n├── 02_todos/                          # Task tracking\n│   └── todo_v001_001_20260316.md\n├── 03_chat_logs/                      # Chat history snapshots\n│   └── 2026-03-16_12-05.md\n├── 04_knowledge/                      # Extracted knowledge/summaries\n│   ├── memory-classification-and-cleaning_2026-03-16.md\n│   └── memorytree-vault-architecture_2026-03-16.md\n└── 06_transcripts/                    # AI session transcripts (main)\n    ├── raw/                           # Original JSONL\n    │   ├── codex/2026/03/*.jsonl\n    │   └── claude/2026/03/*.jsonl\n    ├── clean/                         # Cleaned markdown\n    │   ├── codex/2026/03/*.md\n    │   └── claude/2026/03/*.md\n    └── manifests/                     # Metadata JSON\n        ├── codex/2026/03/*.json\n        └── claude/2026/03/*.json\n```\n\n**Example Manifest** (019cf4b1-5d2d-7d20-b373-a69ade3bc5c3__0ebaa458.json):\n- Client: codex\n- Session ID: 019cf4b1-5d2d-7d20-b373-a69ade3bc5c3\n- Message count: 203\n- Tool event count: 748\n- Import timestamp: 2026-03-16T08:32:09Z\n- SHA256: 0ebaa45828828a35237af35509c3dd72dab402be55e9b683039bf7a1615da5a5\n\n**Example Clean Markdown** (first 100 lines):\n- YAML front matter with metadata\n- Numbered messages with timestamps\n- Indented text content\n- Supports multiple clients with their specific message structure\n\n### 6. **Test Suite** (17 test files)\n\n**Patterns**:\n- Uses Vitest with beforeEach/afterEach setup\n- Mocks `node:os.homedir()` to temp directories (prevents pollution)\n- Test fixtures in tmpdir\n- Examples:\n  - `config.test.ts`: roundtrip save/load, interval parsing, project registration\n  - `heartbeat.test.ts`: sensitive pattern detection (API keys, tokens, passwords)\n  - `parse.test.ts`: (not shown but exists)\n  - `import.test.ts`: (not shown but exists)\n  - `lock.test.ts`: (not shown but exists)\n  - `alert.test.ts`: (not shown but exists)\n\n**Test Pattern Example** (config.test.ts):\n```typescript\nbeforeEach(() => { tmpDir = mkdtempSync(...) })\nafterEach(() => { rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true }) })\n\ndescribe('loadConfig', () => {\n  it('returns defaults when file missing', () => { ... })\n  it('parses valid TOML', () => { ... })\n  it('uses defaults for invalid fields', () => { ... })\n})\n```\n\n### 7. **Architecture Notes**\n\n**Immutability Principle**:\n- Config updates return new objects: `registerProject(cfg, path)` → new Config\n- Alert operations create new arrays: `writeAlert()` reads, modifies copy, writes\n- Failure counts: same pattern\n\n**Stateless & Idempotent**:\n- Heartbeat: single-shot execution, no state between runs\n- Lock prevents concurrent execution\n- Manifest deduplication by SHA256 prevents re-imports\n\n**Platform Support**:\n- Windows: tasklist for PID checking, handles backslashes in paths\n- Unix: kill -0 for PID checking\n- Path normalization: toPosixPath converts backslashes to forward slashes\n\n**Security**:\n- Sensitive pattern detection before import\n- Alerts on matches (no upload)\n- File hashing prevents tampering detection\n- Input validation throughout\n\n### 8. **Key Implementation Details**\n\n**Timestamp Handling**:\n- Unix seconds → ISO 8601 conversion\n- Fallback to file mtime if metadata missing\n- Earliest timestamp detection for session start\n- Partition by YYYY/MM for directory structure\n\n**Text Extraction**:\n- Multi-format support: string, nested objects, arrays\n- Deduplication by timestamp + content hash\n- Paragraph joining with double newlines\n- Summarization with truncation (default 180 chars)\n\n**Git Integration**:\n- Works with any git repo with Memory/ subdirectory\n- Commits only if changes exist in Memory/\n- Automatic retry on push failure\n- Respects auto_push config flag\n\nThis is a complete, production-grade TypeScript implementation of a transcript management system with heartbeat scheduling, multi-client parsing, and persistent state management.",
    "type": "text"
  },

Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * MemoryTree CLI — unified entry point.
     3→ * Registers all subcommands via commander.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { Command } from 'commander'
     7→
     8→const program = new Command()
     9→
    10→program
    11→  .name('memorytree')
    12→  .description('MemoryTree — transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity')
    13→  .version('0.1.0')
    14→
    15→// ── init ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16→
    17→program
    18→  .command('init')
    19→  .description('Initialize a MemoryTree workspace in a repository')
    20→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    21→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project name', 'this project')
    22→  .option('--goal-summary <text>', 'Initial goal summary', 'Describe the long-term project goal here.')
    23→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Template locale: auto, en, or zh-cn', 'auto')
    24→  .option('--date <date>', 'Override date as YYYY-MM-DD')
    25→  .option('--time <time>', 'Override time as HH:MM')
    26→  .option('--skip-agents', 'Deprecated — use upgrade instead')
    27→  .option('--force', 'Overwrite existing generated files')
    28→  .action(async (opts) => {
    29→    const { cmdInit } = await import('./cmd-init.js')
    30→    process.exitCode = cmdInit({
    31→      root: opts.root,
    32→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    33→      goalSummary: opts.goalSummary,
    34→      locale: opts.locale,
    35→      date: opts.date ?? '',
    36→      time: opts.time ?? '',
    37→      skipAgents: opts.skipAgents ?? false,
    38→      force: opts.force ?? false,
    39→    })
    40→  })
    41→
    42→// ── upgrade ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    43→
    44→program
    45→  .command('upgrade')
    46→  .description('Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree without overwriting existing policy')
    47→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    48→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project name', 'this project')
    49→  .option('--goal-summary <text>', 'Fallback goal summary', 'Describe the long-term project goal here.')
    50→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Requested locale: auto, en, or zh-cn', 'auto')
    51→  .option('--date <date>', 'Override date as YYYY-MM-DD')
    52→  .option('--time <time>', 'Override time as HH:MM')
    53→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
    54→  .action(async (opts) => {
    55→    const { cmdUpgrade } = await import('./cmd-upgrade.js')
    56→    process.exitCode = cmdUpgrade({
    57→      root: opts.root,
    58→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    59→      goalSummary: opts.goalSummary,
    60→      locale: opts.locale,
    61→      date: opts.date ?? '',
    62→      time: opts.time ?? '',
    63→      format: opts.format,
    64→    })
    65→  })
    66→
    67→// ── import ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→
    69→program
    70→  .command('import')
    71→  .description('Import one local transcript into MemoryTree archives')
    72→  .requiredOption('--source <path>', 'Raw transcript source file path')
    73→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    74→  .option('--client <client>', 'Transcript client: auto, codex, claude, gemini', 'auto')
    75→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
    76→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
    77→  .option('--raw-upload-permission <perm>', 'Permission: not-set, approved, denied', 'not-set')
    78→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
    79→  .action(async (opts) => {
    80→    const { cmdImport } = await import('./cmd-import.js')
    81→    process.exitCode = await cmdImport({
    82→      root: opts.root,
    83→      source: opts.source,
    84→      client: opts.client,
    85→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    86→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
    87→      rawUploadPermission: opts.rawUploadPermission,
    88→      format: opts.format,
    89→    })
    90→  })
    91→
    92→// ── discover ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    93→
    94→program
    95→  .command('discover')
    96→  .description('Discover and import local AI transcripts')
    97→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    98→  .option('--client <client>', 'Client filter: all, codex, claude, gemini', 'all')
    99→  .option('--scope <scope>', 'Scope: current-project or all-projects', 'all-projects')
   100→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
   101→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
   102→  .option('--raw-upload-permission <perm>', 'Permission: not-set, approved, denied', 'not-set')
   103→  .option('--limit <n>', 'Limit discovered sources', '0')
   104→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   105→  .action(async (opts) => {
   106→    const { cmdDiscover } = await import('./cmd-discover.js')
   107→    process.exitCode = await cmdDiscover({
   108→      root: opts.root,
   109→      client: opts.client,
   110→      scope: opts.scope,
   111→      projectName: opts.projectName,
   112→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
   113→      rawUploadPermission: opts.rawUploadPermission,
   114→      limit: parseInt(opts.limit, 10) || 0,
   115→      format: opts.format,
   116→    })
   117→  })
   118→
   119→// ── locale ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   120→
   121→program
   122→  .command('locale')
   123→  .description('Detect the effective locale for a repository')
   124→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
   125→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Requested locale value', 'auto')
   126→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   127→  .action(async (opts) => {
   128→    const { cmdLocale } = await import('./cmd-locale.js')
   129→    process.exitCode = cmdLocale({
   130→      root: opts.root,
   131→      locale: opts.locale,
   132→      format: opts.format,
   133→    })
   134→  })
   135→
   136→// ── recall ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   137→
   138→program
   139→  .command('recall')
   140→  .description('On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall')
   141→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
   142→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project label', '')
   143→  .option('--global-root <path>', 'Override global transcript root')
   144→  .option('--activation-time <time>', 'ISO timestamp of session activation')
   145→  .option('--format <format>', 'Output format: text or json', 'text')
   146→  .action(async (opts) => {
   147→    const { cmdRecall } = await import('./cmd-recall.js')
   148→    process.exitCode = await cmdRecall({
   149→      root: opts.root,
   150→      projectName: opts.projectName,
   151→      globalRoot: opts.globalRoot ?? '',
   152→      activationTime: opts.activationTime ?? '',
   153→      format: opts.format,
   154→    })
   155→  })
   156→
   157→// ── daemon ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   158→
   159→const daemon = program
   160→  .command('daemon')
   161→  .description('Manage the MemoryTree heartbeat lifecycle')
   162→
   163→daemon
   164→  .command('install')
   165→  .description('Register heartbeat with the OS scheduler')
   166→  .option('--interval <interval>', 'Override heartbeat interval (e.g., "5m")')
   167→  .option('--auto-push <bool>', 'Override auto_push setting (true/false)')
   168→  .action(async (opts) => {
   169→    const { cmdInstall } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   170→    process.exitCode = cmdInstall({
   171→      interval: opts.interval,
   172→      autoPush: opts.autoPush,
   173→    })
   174→  })
   175→
   176→daemon
   177→  .command('uninstall')
   178→  .description('Remove the heartbeat scheduled task')
   179→  .action(async () => {
   180→    const { cmdUninstall } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   181→    process.exitCode = cmdUninstall()
   182→  })
   183→
   184→daemon
   185→  .command('run-once')
   186→  .description('Execute a single heartbeat cycle now')
   187→  .action(async () => {
   188→    const { cmdRunOnce } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   189→    process.exitCode = await cmdRunOnce()
   190→  })
   191→
   192→daemon
   193→  .command('watch')
   194→  .description('Continuous heartbeat loop (development only)')
   195→  .option('--interval <interval>', 'Override interval')
   196→  .action(async (opts) => {
   197→    const { cmdWatch } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   198→    process.exitCode = await cmdWatch({ interval: opts.interval })
   199→  })
   200→
   201→daemon
   202→  .command('status')
   203→  .description('Show heartbeat registration and lock state')
   204→  .action(async () => {
   205→    const { cmdStatus } = await import('./cmd-daemon.js')
   206→    process.exitCode = cmdStatus()
   207→  })
   208→
   209→program.parse()
   210→

Tool Result #14 Read toolu_01TViF3eX1JjghEaThyPR7yv 09:49:19
Read output=1→/** 2→ * Load, validate, and manage ~/.memorytree/config.toml. 3→ * Port of scripts/_config_utils.py 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync } f...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Load, validate, and manage ~/.memorytree/config.toml.
     3→ * Port of scripts/_config_utils.py
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { homedir } from 'node:os'
     8→import { dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'
     9→
    10→import { parse as parseToml } from 'smol-toml'
    11→
    12→import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'
    13→
    14→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→// Constants
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→const DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = '5m'
    19→const DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH = true
    20→const DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL = 'info'
    21→const VALID_LOG_LEVELS: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error'])
    22→
    23→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→// Types
    25→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→
    27→export interface ProjectEntry {
    28→  readonly path: string
    29→  readonly name: string
    30→}
    31→
    32→export interface Config {
    33→  readonly heartbeat_interval: string
    34→  readonly watch_dirs: readonly string[]
    35→  readonly projects: readonly ProjectEntry[]
    36→  readonly auto_push: boolean
    37→  readonly log_level: string
    38→}
    39→
    40→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→// Path helpers
    42→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→
    44→export function memorytreeRoot(): string {
    45→  return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree')
    46→}
    47→
    48→export function configPath(): string {
    49→  return resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'config.toml')
    50→}
    51→
    52→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    53→// Load / Save
    54→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    55→
    56→export function loadConfig(): Config {
    57→  const path = configPath()
    58→  if (!existsSync(path)) {
    59→    return defaultConfig()
    60→  }
    61→  let raw: Record<string, unknown>
    62→  try {
    63→    const text = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    64→    raw = parseToml(text) as Record<string, unknown>
    65→  } catch {
    66→    return defaultConfig()
    67→  }
    68→  return parseRaw(raw)
    69→}
    70→
    71→export function saveConfig(cfg: Config): void {
    72→  const path = configPath()
    73→  mkdirSync(dirname(path), { recursive: true })
    74→
    75→  const lines: string[] = [
    76→    `heartbeat_interval = ${tomlString(cfg.heartbeat_interval)}`,
    77→    `auto_push = ${cfg.auto_push ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
    78→    `log_level = ${tomlString(cfg.log_level)}`,
    79→  ]
    80→
    81→  if (cfg.watch_dirs.length > 0) {
    82→    const items = cfg.watch_dirs.map(d => tomlString(d)).join(', ')
    83→    lines.push(`watch_dirs = [${items}]`)
    84→  } else {
    85→    lines.push('watch_dirs = []')
    86→  }
    87→
    88→  lines.push('')
    89→  for (const project of cfg.projects) {
    90→    lines.push('[[projects]]')
    91→    lines.push(`path = ${tomlString(project.path)}`)
    92→    if (project.name) {
    93→      lines.push(`name = ${tomlString(project.name)}`)
    94→    }
    95→    lines.push('')
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  writeFileSync(path, lines.join('\n') + '\n', 'utf-8')
    99→}
   100→
   101→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   102→// Interval parsing
   103→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   104→
   105→export function intervalToSeconds(interval: string): number {
   106→  const match = interval.trim().toLowerCase().match(/^(\d+)\s*(s|m|h)$/)
   107→  if (!match) {
   108→    return intervalToSeconds(DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
   109→  }
   110→  const value = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)
   111→  const unit = match[2]!
   112→  if (value <= 0) {
   113→    return intervalToSeconds(DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
   114→  }
   115→  const multiplier: Record<string, number> = { s: 1, m: 60, h: 3600 }
   116→  return value * (multiplier[unit] ?? 60)
   117→}
   118→
   119→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   120→// Project registration
   121→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   122→
   123→export function registerProject(cfg: Config, repoPath: string): Config {
   124→  const resolved = toPosixPath(resolve(repoPath))
   125→  for (const entry of cfg.projects) {
   126→    if (toPosixPath(resolve(entry.path)) === resolved) {
   127→      return cfg
   128→    }
   129→  }
   130→  const name = resolved.split('/').pop() ?? ''
   131→  return {
   132→    ...cfg,
   133→    projects: [...cfg.projects, { path: resolved, name }],
   134→  }
   135→}
   136→
   137→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   138→// Internal helpers
   139→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   140→
   141→function defaultConfig(): Config {
   142→  return {
   143→    heartbeat_interval: DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,
   144→    watch_dirs: [],
   145→    projects: [],
   146→    auto_push: DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH,
   147→    log_level: DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL,
   148→  }
   149→}
   150→
   151→function parseRaw(raw: Record<string, unknown>): Config {
   152→  let interval = raw['heartbeat_interval']
   153→  if (typeof interval !== 'string' || !isValidInterval(interval)) {
   154→    interval = DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
   155→  }
   156→
   157→  let autoPush = raw['auto_push']
   158→  if (typeof autoPush !== 'boolean') {
   159→    autoPush = DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH
   160→  }
   161→
   162→  let logLevel = raw['log_level']
   163→  if (typeof logLevel !== 'string' || !VALID_LOG_LEVELS.has(logLevel.toLowerCase())) {
   164→    logLevel = DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL
   165→  }
   166→  logLevel = (logLevel as string).toLowerCase()
   167→
   168→  const watchDirs: string[] = []
   169→  const rawDirs = raw['watch_dirs']
   170→  if (Array.isArray(rawDirs)) {
   171→    for (const d of rawDirs) {
   172→      if (typeof d === 'string') watchDirs.push(d)
   173→    }
   174→  }
   175→
   176→  const projects: ProjectEntry[] = []
   177→  const rawProjects = raw['projects']
   178→  if (Array.isArray(rawProjects)) {
   179→    for (const entry of rawProjects) {
   180→      if (entry !== null && typeof entry === 'object' && !Array.isArray(entry)) {
   181→        const rec = entry as Record<string, unknown>
   182→        if (typeof rec['path'] === 'string') {
   183→          projects.push({
   184→            path: rec['path'] as string,
   185→            name: String(rec['name'] ?? ''),
   186→          })
   187→        }
   188→      }
   189→    }
   190→  }
   191→
   192→  return {
   193→    heartbeat_interval: interval as string,
   194→    watch_dirs: watchDirs,
   195→    projects,
   196→    auto_push: autoPush as boolean,
   197→    log_level: logLevel as string,
   198→  }
   199→}
   200→
   201→function isValidInterval(value: string): boolean {
   202→  const match = value.trim().toLowerCase().match(/^(\d+)\s*(s|m|h)$/)
   203→  if (!match) return false
   204→  return parseInt(match[1]!, 10) > 0
   205→}
   206→
   207→function tomlString(value: string): string {
   208→  const escaped = value.replace(/\\/g, '\\\\').replace(/"/g, '\\"')
   209→  return `"${escaped}"`
   210→}
   211→

Tool Result #15 Read toolu_01E9wE37M8aXPPLc8VKdr9pe 09:49:19
Read output=1→/** 2→ * MemoryTree heartbeat — single execution, stateless, idempotent. 3→ * Port of scripts/heartbeat.py 4→ */ 5→ 6→import type { ParsedTranscript } from '../types/transcrip...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * MemoryTree heartbeat — single execution, stateless, idempotent.
     3→ * Port of scripts/heartbeat.py
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import type { ParsedTranscript } from '../types/transcript.js'
     7→import { discoverSourceFiles, transcriptMatchesRepo } from '../transcript/discover.js'
     8→import { importTranscript, transcriptHasContent } from '../transcript/import.js'
     9→import { parseTranscript } from '../transcript/parse.js'
    10→import { slugify } from '../transcript/common.js'
    11→import { defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot } from '../transcript/discover.js'
    12→import type { Config } from './config.js'
    13→import { loadConfig } from './config.js'
    14→import { acquireLock, releaseLock } from './lock.js'
    15→import { resetFailureCount, writeAlert, writeAlertWithThreshold } from './alert.js'
    16→import type { LogLevel } from './log.js'
    17→import { getLogger, setupLogging } from './log.js'
    18→import { git } from '../utils/exec.js'
    19→import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'
    20→import { basename, resolve } from 'node:path'
    21→import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
    22→
    23→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→// Sensitive pattern detection
    25→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→
    27→const SENSITIVE_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
    28→  /(?:api[_-]?key|apikey)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i,
    29→  /(?:password|passwd|pwd)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i,
    30→  /(?:secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i,
    31→  /(?:sk-|pk_live_|sk_live_|ghp_|gho_|glpat-)\S{10,}/,
    32→  /Bearer\s+\S{20,}/i,
    33→]
    34→
    35→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36→// Main entry point
    37→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→
    39→export async function main(): Promise<number> {
    40→  const config = loadConfig()
    41→  setupLogging(config.log_level as LogLevel)
    42→  const logger = getLogger()
    43→
    44→  if (!acquireLock()) {
    45→    logger.info('Another heartbeat instance is running. Exiting.')
    46→    writeAlert('global', 'lock_held', 'Heartbeat exited: another instance held the lock.')
    47→    return 0
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  try {
    51→    return await runHeartbeat(config)
    52→  } finally {
    53→    releaseLock()
    54→  }
    55→}
    56→
    57→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    58→// Heartbeat orchestration
    59→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→
    61→export async function runHeartbeat(config: Config): Promise<number> {
    62→  const logger = getLogger()
    63→
    64→  if (config.projects.length === 0) {
    65→    logger.info('No projects registered in config.toml. Nothing to do.')
    66→    return 0
    67→  }
    68→
    69→  logger.info(`Heartbeat started. ${config.projects.length} project(s) registered.`)
    70→
    71→  for (const entry of config.projects) {
    72→    const projectPath = resolve(entry.path)
    73→    if (!existsSync(projectPath)) {
    74→      logger.warn(`Project path does not exist, skipping: ${projectPath}`)
    75→      continue
    76→    }
    77→    try {
    78→      await processProject(config, projectPath, entry.name || basename(projectPath))
    79→    } catch (err: unknown) {
    80→      logger.exception(`Error processing project: ${projectPath}`, err)
    81→      writeAlertWithThreshold(
    82→        toPosixPath(projectPath),
    83→        'push_failed',
    84→        `Heartbeat error for project: ${basename(projectPath)}`,
    85→      )
    86→    }
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  logger.info('Heartbeat finished.')
    90→  return 0
    91→}
    92→
    93→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    94→// Per-project processing
    95→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    96→
    97→export async function processProject(config: Config, projectPath: string, projectName: string): Promise<void> {
    98→  const logger = getLogger()
    99→  const repoSlug = slugify(projectName, 'project')
   100→  const globalRoot = defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot()
   101→
   102→  const discovered = discoverSourceFiles()
   103→  let importedCount = 0
   104→
   105→  for (const [client, source] of discovered) {
   106→    let parsed: ParsedTranscript
   107→    try {
   108→      parsed = parseTranscript(client, source)
   109→    } catch {
   110→      logger.debug(`Failed to parse ${source}, skipping.`)
   111→      continue
   112→    }
   113→
   114→    if (!transcriptHasContent(parsed)) continue
   115→    if (!transcriptMatchesRepo(parsed, projectPath, repoSlug)) continue
   116→
   117→    scanSensitive(parsed, projectPath)
   118→
   119→    try {
   120→      await importTranscript(parsed, projectPath, globalRoot, repoSlug, 'not-set', true)
   121→      importedCount++
   122→    } catch {
   123→      logger.exception(`Failed to import transcript: ${source}`)
   124→    }
   125→  }
   126→
   127→  if (importedCount === 0) {
   128→    logger.info(`[${projectName}] No new transcripts to import.`)
   129→    return
   130→  }
   131→
   132→  logger.info(`[${projectName}] Imported ${importedCount} transcript(s).`)
   133→  gitCommitAndPush(config, projectPath, projectName, importedCount)
   134→}
   135→
   136→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   137→// Sensitive pattern scanning
   138→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   139→
   140→export function scanSensitive(parsed: ParsedTranscript, projectPath: string): void {
   141→  const logger = getLogger()
   142→  for (const msg of parsed.messages) {
   143→    for (const pattern of SENSITIVE_PATTERNS) {
   144→      if (pattern.test(msg.text)) {
   145→        logger.warn(
   146→          `Sensitive pattern detected in transcript ${parsed.source_path} (project: ${basename(projectPath)}, role: ${msg.role})`,
   147→        )
   148→        writeAlert(
   149→          toPosixPath(projectPath),
   150→          'sensitive_match',
   151→          `Sensitive pattern in transcript: ${basename(parsed.source_path)}`,
   152→        )
   153→        return
   154→      }
   155→    }
   156→  }
   157→}
   158→
   159→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   160→// Git operations
   161→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   162→
   163→export function gitCommitAndPush(config: Config, projectPath: string, projectName: string, count: number): void {
   164→  const logger = getLogger()
   165→
   166→  const status = git(projectPath, 'status', '--porcelain', 'Memory/')
   167→  if (!status.trim()) {
   168→    logger.info(`[${projectName}] No git changes in Memory/.`)
   169→    return
   170→  }
   171→
   172→  git(projectPath, 'add', 'Memory/')
   173→  git(projectPath, 'commit', '-m', `memorytree(transcripts): import ${count} transcript(s)`)
   174→  logger.info(`[${projectName}] Committed ${count} transcript import(s).`)
   175→
   176→  if (!config.auto_push) {
   177→    logger.info(`[${projectName}] auto_push disabled, skipping push.`)
   178→    return
   179→  }
   180→
   181→  const remotes = git(projectPath, 'remote')
   182→  if (!remotes.trim()) {
   183→    logger.warn(`[${projectName}] No git remote configured, skipping push.`)
   184→    writeAlert(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'no_remote', 'Push skipped: no Git remote configured.')
   185→    return
   186→  }
   187→
   188→  if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
   189→    logger.warn(`[${projectName}] Push failed, retrying once...`)
   190→    if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
   191→      logger.error(`[${projectName}] Push failed after retry.`)
   192→      writeAlertWithThreshold(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed', 'Push failed after retry.')
   193→      return
   194→    }
   195→  }
   196→
   197→  resetFailureCount(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed')
   198→}
   199→
   200→export function tryPush(projectPath: string, projectName: string): boolean {
   201→  try {
   202→    git(projectPath, 'push')
   203→    getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Pushed successfully.`)
   204→    return true
   205→  } catch {
   206→    return false
   207→  }
   208→}
   209→

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, rmSync, mkdirSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Mock homedir so all path helpers point to a temp directory
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→let tmpDir: string
    11→
    12→vi.mock('node:os', async () => {
    13→  const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('node:os')>('node:os')
    14→  return {
    15→    ...actual,
    16→    homedir: () => tmpDir,
    17→  }
    18→})
    19→
    20→// Import after mock setup so configPath() / memorytreeRoot() use the mock
    21→import {
    22→  loadConfig,
    23→  saveConfig,
    24→  intervalToSeconds,
    25→  registerProject,
    26→  configPath,
    27→  memorytreeRoot,
    28→} from '../../src/heartbeat/config.js'
    29→
    30→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31→// Setup / Teardown
    32→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    33→
    34→beforeEach(() => {
    35→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'cfg-test-'))
    36→})
    37→
    38→afterEach(() => {
    39→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    40→})
    41→
    42→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→// configPath / memorytreeRoot
    44→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→
    46→describe('configPath / memorytreeRoot', () => {
    47→  it('memorytreeRoot returns <home>/.memorytree', () => {
    48→    const root = memorytreeRoot()
    49→    expect(root).toContain('.memorytree')
    50→    expect(root.startsWith(tmpDir)).toBe(true)
    51→  })
    52→
    53→  it('configPath returns <home>/.memorytree/config.toml', () => {
    54→    const path = configPath()
    55→    expect(path).toContain('config.toml')
    56→    expect(path.startsWith(tmpDir)).toBe(true)
    57→  })
    58→})
    59→
    60→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    61→// loadConfig
    62→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    63→
    64→describe('loadConfig', () => {
    65→  it('returns defaults when config file does not exist', () => {
    66→    const cfg = loadConfig()
    67→    expect(cfg.heartbeat_interval).toBe('5m')
    68→    expect(cfg.auto_push).toBe(true)
    69→    expect(cfg.log_level).toBe('info')
    70→    expect(cfg.watch_dirs).toEqual([])
    71→    expect(cfg.projects).toEqual([])
    72→  })
    73→
    74→  it('parses valid TOML file', () => {
    75→    const path = configPath()
    76→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })
    77→    writeFileSync(
    78→      path,
    79→      [
    80→        'heartbeat_interval = "10m"',
    81→        'auto_push = false',
    82→        'log_level = "debug"',
    83→        'watch_dirs = ["/home/user/repos"]',
    84→        '',
    85→        '[[projects]]',
    86→        'path = "/home/user/project-a"',
    87→        'name = "project-a"',
    88→        '',
    89→      ].join('\n'),
    90→    )
    91→
    92→    const cfg = loadConfig()
    93→    expect(cfg.heartbeat_interval).toBe('10m')
    94→    expect(cfg.auto_push).toBe(false)
    95→    expect(cfg.log_level).toBe('debug')
    96→    expect(cfg.watch_dirs).toEqual(['/home/user/repos'])
    97→    expect(cfg.projects).toHaveLength(1)
    98→    expect(cfg.projects[0]!.path).toBe('/home/user/project-a')
    99→    expect(cfg.projects[0]!.name).toBe('project-a')
   100→  })
   101→
   102→  it('returns defaults for invalid TOML', () => {
   103→    const path = configPath()
   104→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })
   105→    writeFileSync(path, '<<<not valid toml>>>')
   106→
   107→    const cfg = loadConfig()
   108→    expect(cfg.heartbeat_interval).toBe('5m')
   109→    expect(cfg.auto_push).toBe(true)
   110→  })
   111→
   112→  it('uses defaults for missing or invalid fields', () => {
   113→    const path = configPath()
   114→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, '.memorytree'), { recursive: true })
   115→    // Only set heartbeat_interval; other fields should default
   116→    writeFileSync(path, 'heartbeat_interval = "1h"\n')
   117→
   118→    const cfg = loadConfig()
   119→    expect(cfg.heartbeat_interval).toBe('1h')
   120→    expect(cfg.auto_push).toBe(true)
   121→    expect(cfg.log_level).toBe('info')
   122→    expect(cfg.watch_dirs).toEqual([])
   123→    expect(cfg.projects).toEqual([])
   124→  })
   125→})
   126→
   127→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   128→// saveConfig
   129→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   130→
   131→describe('saveConfig', () => {
   132→  it('roundtrip: save then load yields equivalent config', () => {
   133→    const original = {
   134→      heartbeat_interval: '10m',
   135→      auto_push: false,
   136→      log_level: 'debug',
   137→      watch_dirs: ['/repos/a', '/repos/b'],
   138→      projects: [
   139→        { path: '/home/user/project-a', name: 'project-a' },
   140→        { path: '/home/user/project-b', name: 'project-b' },
   141→      ],
   142→    } as const
   143→
   144→    saveConfig(original)
   145→    const loaded = loadConfig()
   146→
   147→    expect(loaded.heartbeat_interval).toBe(original.heartbeat_interval)
   148→    expect(loaded.auto_push).toBe(original.auto_push)
   149→    expect(loaded.log_level).toBe(original.log_level)
   150→    expect(loaded.watch_dirs).toEqual([...original.watch_dirs])
   151→    expect(loaded.projects).toHaveLength(2)
   152→    expect(loaded.projects[0]!.path).toBe(original.projects[0]!.path)
   153→    expect(loaded.projects[1]!.name).toBe(original.projects[1]!.name)
   154→  })
   155→
   156→  it('serializes empty projects list', () => {
   157→    const cfg = {
   158→      heartbeat_interval: '5m',
   159→      auto_push: true,
   160→      log_level: 'info',
   161→      watch_dirs: [],
   162→      projects: [],
   163→    } as const
   164→
   165→    saveConfig(cfg)
   166→
   167→    const text = readFileSync(configPath(), 'utf-8')
   168→    expect(text).toContain('heartbeat_interval = "5m"')
   169→    expect(text).toContain('auto_push = true')
   170→    expect(text).not.toContain('[[projects]]')
   171→  })
   172→})
   173→
   174→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   175→// intervalToSeconds
   176→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   177→
   178→describe('intervalToSeconds', () => {
   179→  it('parses minutes: 5m -> 300', () => {
   180→    expect(intervalToSeconds('5m')).toBe(300)
   181→  })
   182→
   183→  it('parses hours: 1h -> 3600', () => {
   184→    expect(intervalToSeconds('1h')).toBe(3600)
   185→  })
   186→
   187→  it('parses seconds: 30s -> 30', () => {
   188→    expect(intervalToSeconds('30s')).toBe(30)
   189→  })
   190→
   191→  it('returns default (300) for invalid input', () => {
   192→    expect(intervalToSeconds('invalid')).toBe(300)
   193→  })
   194→
   195→  it('returns default (300) for zero value', () => {
   196→    expect(intervalToSeconds('0m')).toBe(300)
   197→  })
   198→
   199→  it('handles leading/trailing whitespace', () => {
   200→    expect(intervalToSeconds('  10m  ')).toBe(600)
   201→  })
   202→
   203→  it('is case-insensitive', () => {
   204→    expect(intervalToSeconds('2H')).toBe(7200)
   205→  })
   206→})
   207→
   208→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   209→// registerProject
   210→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   211→
   212→describe('registerProject', () => {
   213→  it('adds a new project to an empty list', () => {
   214→    const cfg = {
   215→      heartbeat_interval: '5m',
   216→      auto_push: true,
   217→      log_level: 'info',
   218→      watch_dirs: [],
   219→      projects: [],
   220→    } as const
   221→
   222→    const updated = registerProject(cfg, tmpDir)
   223→    expect(updated.projects).toHaveLength(1)
   224→    expect(updated.projects[0]!.path).toBeTruthy()
   225→    // Name derived from last path segment
   226→    expect(updated.projects[0]!.name).toBeTruthy()
   227→  })
   228→
   229→  it('skips duplicate project (by resolved path)', () => {
   230→    const cfg = {
   231→      heartbeat_interval: '5m',
   232→      auto_push: true,
   233→      log_level: 'info',
   234→      watch_dirs: [],
   235→      projects: [],
   236→    } as const
   237→
   238→    const updated1 = registerProject(cfg, tmpDir)
   239→    const updated2 = registerProject(updated1, tmpDir)
   240→    expect(updated2.projects).toHaveLength(1)
   241→  })
   242→
   243→  it('does not mutate the original config', () => {
   244→    const cfg = {
   245→      heartbeat_interval: '5m',
   246→      auto_push: true,
   247→      log_level: 'info',
   248→      watch_dirs: [],
   249→      projects: [],
   250→    } as const
   251→
   252→    const updated = registerProject(cfg, tmpDir)
   253→    expect(cfg.projects).toHaveLength(0)
   254→    expect(updated.projects).toHaveLength(1)
   255→  })
   256→})
   257→

1→// Types — ParsedTranscript, Message, ToolEvent interfaces
     2→// To be implemented in Phase 1
     3→
     4→export type Client = 'codex' | 'claude' | 'gemini'
     5→
     6→export interface TranscriptMessage {
     7→  role: string
     8→  text: string
     9→  timestamp: string | null
    10→}
    11→
    12→export interface TranscriptToolEvent {
    13→  summary: string
    14→  timestamp: string | null
    15→}
    16→
    17→export interface ParsedTranscript {
    18→  client: Client
    19→  session_id: string
    20→  title: string
    21→  started_at: string
    22→  cwd: string
    23→  branch: string
    24→  messages: TranscriptMessage[]
    25→  tool_events: TranscriptToolEvent[]
    26→  source_path: string
    27→}
    28→
    29→export interface ManifestEntry {
    30→  client: string
    31→  project: string
    32→  session_id: string
    33→  raw_sha256: string
    34→  title: string
    35→  started_at: string
    36→  imported_at: string
    37→  cwd: string
    38→  branch: string
    39→  raw_source_path: string
    40→  raw_upload_permission: string
    41→  global_raw_path: string
    42→  global_clean_path: string

    49→  cleaning_mode: string
    50→  repo_mirror_enabled: boolean
    51→}

Read output=1→/** 2→ * Transcript import — orchestration, cleaning, and manifest management. 3→ * Port of scripts/_transcript_import.py 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { copyFileSync, existsSync, mkdirSy...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Transcript import — orchestration, cleaning, and manifest management.
     3→ * Port of scripts/_transcript_import.py
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { copyFileSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, appendFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { dirname, extname, join, basename, relative } from 'node:path'
     8→
     9→import type { ManifestEntry, ParsedTranscript } from '../types/transcript.js'
    10→import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'
    11→import {
    12→  loadJson,
    13→  normalizeTimestamp,
    14→  sha256File,
    15→  slugify,
    16→  timestampPartition,
    17→  yamlEscape,
    18→} from './common.js'
    19→import { upsertSearchIndex } from './db.js'
    20→
    21→export async function importTranscript(
    22→  parsed: ParsedTranscript,
    23→  root: string,
    24→  globalRoot: string,
    25→  projectSlug: string,
    26→  rawUploadPermission: string,
    27→  mirrorToRepo = true,
    28→): Promise<ManifestEntry> {
    29→  const importedAt = normalizeTimestamp(new Date())
    30→  const rawSha256 = sha256File(parsed.source_path)
    31→  const sessionLabel = slugify(
    32→    parsed.session_id,
    33→    slugify(stemOf(parsed.source_path), 'session'),
    34→  )
    35→  const artifactStem = `${sessionLabel}__${rawSha256.slice(0, 8)}`
    36→  const sourceSuffix = extname(parsed.source_path) || '.txt'
    37→
    38→  const [yearToken, monthToken] = timestampPartition(parsed.started_at || importedAt)
    39→
    40→  const repoRoot = join(root, 'Memory', '06_transcripts')
    41→  const repoRawPath = join(repoRoot, 'raw', parsed.client, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}${sourceSuffix}`)
    42→  const repoCleanPath = join(repoRoot, 'clean', parsed.client, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.md`)
    43→  const repoManifestPath = join(repoRoot, 'manifests', parsed.client, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.json`)
    44→
    45→  const globalRawPath = join(globalRoot, 'raw', parsed.client, projectSlug, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}${sourceSuffix}`)
    46→  const globalCleanPath = join(globalRoot, 'clean', parsed.client, projectSlug, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.md`)
    47→  const globalManifestPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'manifests', parsed.client, projectSlug, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.json`)
    48→  const globalEventLogPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'sessions.jsonl')
    49→  const globalDbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')
    50→
    51→  for (const p of [globalRawPath, globalCleanPath, globalManifestPath, globalEventLogPath, globalDbPath]) {
    52→    mkdirSync(dirname(p), { recursive: true })
    53→  }
    54→  if (mirrorToRepo) {
    55→    for (const p of [repoRawPath, repoCleanPath, repoManifestPath]) {
    56→      mkdirSync(dirname(p), { recursive: true })
    57→    }
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  if (mirrorToRepo) {
    61→    copyFile(parsed.source_path, repoRawPath)
    62→  }
    63→  copyFile(parsed.source_path, globalRawPath)
    64→
    65→  let manifest: ManifestEntry = {
    66→    client: parsed.client,
    67→    project: projectSlug,
    68→    session_id: parsed.session_id,
    69→    title: parsed.title,
    70→    started_at: parsed.started_at,
    71→    imported_at: importedAt,
    72→    cwd: parsed.cwd,
    73→    branch: parsed.branch,
    74→    raw_source_path: toPosixPath(parsed.source_path),
    75→    raw_sha256: rawSha256,
    76→    raw_upload_permission: rawUploadPermission,
    77→    repo_raw_path: mirrorToRepo ? toPosixPath(relative(root, repoRawPath)) : '',
    78→    repo_clean_path: mirrorToRepo ? toPosixPath(relative(root, repoCleanPath)) : '',
    79→    repo_manifest_path: mirrorToRepo ? toPosixPath(relative(root, repoManifestPath)) : '',
    80→    global_raw_path: toPosixPath(globalRawPath),
    81→    global_clean_path: toPosixPath(globalCleanPath),
    82→    global_manifest_path: toPosixPath(globalManifestPath),
    83→    message_count: parsed.messages.length,
    84→    tool_event_count: parsed.tool_events.length,
    85→    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',
    86→    repo_mirror_enabled: mirrorToRepo,
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  const existingGlobalManifest = loadJson(globalManifestPath)
    90→  manifest = preserveExistingImportTimestamp(existingGlobalManifest, manifest)
    91→
    92→  if (mirrorToRepo) {
    93→    writeCleanMarkdown(parsed, manifest, repoCleanPath)
    94→  }
    95→  writeCleanMarkdown(parsed, manifest, globalCleanPath)
    96→  const manifestRecord = { ...manifest } as Record<string, unknown>
    97→  if (mirrorToRepo) {
    98→    writeJson(repoManifestPath, manifestRecord)
    99→  }
   100→  const appendToEventLog = existingGlobalManifest === null || !deepEqual(existingGlobalManifest, manifestRecord)
   101→  writeJson(globalManifestPath, manifestRecord)
   102→  if (appendToEventLog) {
   103→    appendJsonl(globalEventLogPath, manifestRecord)
   104→  }
   105→  await upsertSearchIndex(globalDbPath, manifest)
   106→
   107→  return manifest
   108→}
   109→
   110→export function transcriptHasContent(parsed: ParsedTranscript): boolean {
   111→  return parsed.messages.length > 0 || parsed.tool_events.length > 0
   112→}
   113→
   114→export function writeCleanMarkdown(parsed: ParsedTranscript, manifest: ManifestEntry, filePath: string): void {
   115→  const lines: string[] = [
   116→    '---',
   117→    `client: ${manifest.client}`,
   118→    `project: ${manifest.project}`,
   119→    `session_id: ${manifest.session_id}`,
   120→    `title: ${yamlEscape(manifest.title)}`,
   121→    `started_at: ${manifest.started_at}`,
   122→    `imported_at: ${manifest.imported_at}`,
   123→    `cwd: ${yamlEscape(manifest.cwd)}`,
   124→    `branch: ${yamlEscape(manifest.branch)}`,
   125→    `raw_source_path: ${yamlEscape(manifest.raw_source_path)}`,
   126→    `raw_sha256: ${manifest.raw_sha256}`,
   127→    `raw_upload_permission: ${manifest.raw_upload_permission}`,
   128→    `cleaning_mode: ${manifest.cleaning_mode}`,
   129→    '---',
   130→    '',
   131→    `# ${manifest.title || manifest.session_id}`,
   132→    '',
   133→    '## Metadata',
   134→    `- Client: \`${manifest.client}\``,
   135→    `- Project: \`${manifest.project}\``,
   136→    `- Session ID: \`${manifest.session_id}\``,
   137→    `- Started At: \`${manifest.started_at}\``,
   138→    `- Imported At: \`${manifest.imported_at}\``,
   139→    `- Raw SHA256: \`${manifest.raw_sha256}\``,
   140→    `- Raw Source: \`${manifest.raw_source_path}\``,
   141→    `- Repo Raw Path: \`${manifest.repo_raw_path}\``,
   142→    `- Repo Clean Path: \`${manifest.repo_clean_path}\``,
   143→    '',
   144→    '## Messages',
   145→  ]
   146→
   147→  if (parsed.messages.length > 0) {
   148→    for (let i = 0; i < parsed.messages.length; i++) {
   149→      const message = parsed.messages[i]!
   150→      lines.push(
   151→        `### ${i + 1}. ${message.role}`,
   152→        `- Timestamp: \`${message.timestamp ?? manifest.started_at}\``,
   153→        '',
   154→        message.text,
   155→        '',
   156→      )
   157→    }
   158→  } else {
   159→    lines.push(
   160→      'No user or assistant messages were extracted deterministically from the source transcript.',
   161→      '',
   162→    )
   163→  }
   164→
   165→  writeFileSync(filePath, lines.join('\n'), 'utf-8')
   166→}
   167→
   168→export function preserveExistingImportTimestamp(
   169→  existing: Record<string, unknown> | null,
   170→  payload: ManifestEntry,
   171→): ManifestEntry {
   172→  if (existing === null) return payload
   173→  const payloadRecord = { ...payload } as Record<string, unknown>
   174→  if (!deepEqual(manifestSignature(existing), manifestSignature(payloadRecord))) return payload
   175→  const importedAt = existing['imported_at']
   176→  if (typeof importedAt === 'string' && importedAt) {
   177→    return { ...payload, imported_at: importedAt }
   178→  }
   179→  return payload
   180→}
   181→
   182→export function manifestSignature(payload: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> {
   183→  const result: Record<string, unknown> = {}
   184→  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(payload)) {
   185→    if (key !== 'imported_at') {
   186→      result[key] = value
   187→    }
   188→  }
   189→  return result
   190→}
   191→
   192→export function manifestChanged(filePath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>): boolean {
   193→  if (!existsSync(filePath)) return true
   194→  try {
   195→    const current = JSON.parse(readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')) as Record<string, unknown>
   196→    return !deepEqual(current, payload)
   197→  } catch {
   198→    return true
   199→  }
   200→}
   201→
   202→export function writeJson(filePath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>): void {
   203→  writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8')
   204→}
   205→
   206→export function appendJsonl(filePath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>): void {
   207→  appendFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(payload) + '\n', 'utf-8')
   208→}
   209→
   210→export function copyFile(source: string, destination: string): void {
   211→  if (source === destination) return
   212→  copyFileSync(source, destination)
   213→}
   214→
   215→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   216→// Internal helpers
   217→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   218→
   219→function stemOf(filePath: string): string {
   220→  const base = basename(filePath)
   221→  const ext = extname(base)
   222→  return ext ? base.slice(0, -ext.length) : base
   223→}
   224→
   225→function deepEqual(a: unknown, b: unknown): boolean {
   226→  if (a === b) return true
   227→  if (a === null || b === null || typeof a !== typeof b) return false
   228→  if (typeof a !== 'object') return false
   229→  if (Array.isArray(a) !== Array.isArray(b)) return false
   230→  const aObj = a as Record<string, unknown>
   231→  const bObj = b as Record<string, unknown>
   232→  const aKeys = Object.keys(aObj)
   233→  const bKeys = Object.keys(bObj)
   234→  if (aKeys.length !== bKeys.length) return false
   235→  return aKeys.every(key => deepEqual(aObj[key], bObj[key]))
   236→}
   237→

1→/**
     2→ * Transcript parsing — client inference, dispatch, and format-specific parsers.
     3→ * Port of scripts/_transcript_parse.py
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { readFileSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { basename, extname } from 'node:path'
     8→
     9→import type { Client, ParsedTranscript, TranscriptMessage, TranscriptToolEvent } from '../types/transcript.js'
    10→import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'
    11→import {
    12→  SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES,
    13→  TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES,
    14→  TOOL_RESULT_TYPES,
    15→  TOOL_USE_TYPES,
    16→  deduplicateMessages,
    17→  deduplicateToolEvents,
    18→  earliestTimestamp,
    19→  ensureDict,
    20→  ensureList,
    21→  extractGeminiText,
    22→  extractTextBlocks,
    23→  findFirstMappingWithKeys,
    24→  getNested,
    25→  joinParagraphs,
    26→  loadJsonl,
    27→  normalizeTimestamp,
    28→  summarizeValue,
    29→} from './common.js'
    30→
    31→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→// Client inference + dispatch
    33→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→
    35→export function inferClient(client: string, sourcePath: string): Client {
    36→  if (client !== 'auto') {
    37→    return client as Client
    38→  }
    39→  const normalized = toPosixPath(sourcePath).toLowerCase()
    40→  const fileName = basename(sourcePath).toLowerCase()
    41→  if (normalized.includes('/.codex/') || fileName.startsWith('rollout-')) {
    42→    return 'codex'
    43→  }
    44→  if (normalized.includes('/.claude/') || normalized.includes('/projects/')) {
    45→    return 'claude'
    46→  }
    47→  if (normalized.includes('/.gemini/') || normalized.includes('checkpoint')) {
    48→    return 'gemini'
    49→  }
    50→  throw new Error(`could not infer transcript client from source path: ${sourcePath}`)
    51→}
    52→
    53→export function parseTranscript(client: string, sourcePath: string): ParsedTranscript {
    54→  const resolved = inferClient(client, sourcePath)
    55→  if (resolved === 'codex') {
    56→    return parseCodexTranscript(sourcePath)
    57→  }
    58→  if (resolved === 'claude') {
    59→    return parseClaudeTranscript(sourcePath)
    60→  }
    61→  if (resolved === 'gemini') {
    62→    return parseGeminiTranscript(sourcePath)
    63→  }
    64→  throw new Error(`unsupported transcript client: ${resolved}`)
    65→}
    66→
    67→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    68→// Codex parser
    69→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    70→
    71→export function parseCodexTranscript(filePath: string): ParsedTranscript {
    72→  const records = loadJsonl(filePath)
    73→  const stem = basename(filePath, extname(filePath))
    74→  let sessionId = stem
    75→  let title = stem
    76→  let startedAt = normalizeTimestamp(statSync(filePath).mtimeMs / 1000)
    77→  let cwd = ''
    78→  let branch = ''
    79→  // Canonical messages from `payload.type === 'message'` (response_item records).
    80→  const messages: TranscriptMessage[] = []

  "content": "/**\n * Type definitions for the report generation subsystem.\n */\n\nexport interface ReportStats {\n  totalSessions: number\n  totalMessages: number\n  totalToolEvents: number\n  activeDays: number\n  dateRange: { from: string; to: string }\n  clientCounts: Record<string, number>\n  /** 'YYYY-MM-DD' → session count */\n  dayBuckets: Record<string, number>\n  /** 'YYYY-WNN' → message count */\n  weekBuckets: Record<string, number>\n  /** tool name → invocation count */\n  toolCounts: Record<string, number>\n}\n\nexport interface SummaryCache {\n  sha256: string\n  summary: string\n  generated_at: string\n}\n\nexport interface LinkGraph {\n  /** session_id → list of session_ids that reference it */\n  backlinks: Record<string, string[]>\n  /** session_id → list of session_ids it references */\n  forwardLinks: Record<string, string[]>\n}\n\nexport interface SearchIndexEntry {\n  url: string\n  title: string\n  client: string\n  date: string\n  snippet: string\n}\n\nexport interface BuildReportOptions {\n  root: string\n  output: string\n  noAi?: boolean\n  pdf?: boolean\n  model?: string\n}\n"
